Military


Ukraine Conflict News





14 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs: Saudi Arabia Condemns Terrorist Attack on Syrian and US Security Forces SPA 14 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia expresses the Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist attack that targeted security forces of the Syrian Arab Republic and the United States of America during a joint counterterrorism field patrol near Palmyra, resulting in casualties and injuries
  • Trump Vows 'Very Serious' Retaliation Against ISIS After US Soldiers Killed in Syria Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- US President Donald Trump promised on Saturday "very serious" retaliatory actions against Islamic State* following the deaths of US soldiers in an attack in the Syrian city of Palmyra.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Ukraine's Zelensky signals willingness to drop NATO bid in exchange for security guarantees Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has expressed readiness to forgo Ukraine's longstanding ambition to join NATO in exchange for robust and legally binding security assurances from Western allies.
  • Ukraine Ready to Swap NATO Ambition for Security Guarantees Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- Volodymyr Zelensky said on Sunday that Ukraine was ready to abandon the goal of joining NATO if it received security guarantees from individual partner countries, including security assurances from the United States modeled on Article 5 of the NATO charter.
  • MoD decisions expand capabilities for drone-enabled remote mining Ukraine MOD 14 Dec 2025

  • Chinese Diplomat Says US Should First Reduce Its Own Nuclear Arsenal Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- The United States should first of all reduce its own nuclear arsenal, as creation of conditions for nuclear disarmament depends of Washington, the spokesperson of the Chinese embassy in the US, Liu Pengyu, told Sputnik.

  • Newly delivered M1A2T tanks to undergo monthlong tests: Source CNA 14 Dec 2025 -- A batch of 42 M1A2T Abrams tanks delivered by the United States in July will begin a monthlong testing program on Monday at a northern Army base, including multiple live-fire drills using the tanks' 120mm main guns, a source familiar with the matter told CNA on Sunday.

  • INDIAN NAVY TO COMMISSION INAS 335 (OSPREYS) India PIB 14 Dec 2025 -- The Indian Navy will commission its second MH 60R helicopter squadron, INAS 335 (Ospreys) on 17 Dec 2025 at INS Hansa, Goa, in the presence of Adm Dinesh K Tripathi, the Chief of the Naval Staff.

  • Deputy prime minister W. Kosiniak-Kamysz has met with the Polish Armed Forces contingent in Turkey Poland - Ministry of National Defence 14 Dec 2025

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Syrian Foreign Minister al-Shaibani US Dept. of State 14 Dec 2025
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Australian Minister for Foreign Affairs Wong US Dept. of State 14 Dec 2025

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • The Chief of the General Staff Lights Hanukkah Candles With Troops of the 91st Division Israel Defense Forces 14 Dec 2025

  • Over 300 Palestinians Died From Israeli Attacks Since Ceasefire Began Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- The ceasefire agreement between Israel and the Palestinian movement Hamas in the Gaza Strip took effect on October 10.

  • Hamas warns against Israel's plans to 're-engineer' Gaza Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Hamas warns against the Israeli regime's plans to "re-engineer" the Gaza Strip and displace its Palestinian residents, reiterating that the people of Gaza are the only authority capable of managing their own affairs.
  • Hamas says Israel's killing of senior commander threatens Gaza ceasefire Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Hamas chief negotiator Khalil al-Hayya has warned that Israel's targeted assassination of a senior commander of the movement threatens the "viability of the truce" in the besieged Gaza Strip.

  • At least 3 killed in new Israeli strikes across south Lebanon amid ceasefire violations Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- The Israeli military has killed at least three people in southern Lebanon during separate airstrikes after threatening to resume "wide-scale" aggression against the country.

  • Over 1,000 Palestinians died in Gaza while awaiting medical evacuation, says WHO Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- The World Health Organization (WHO) says more than a thousand Palestinians have died in the Gaza Strip while waiting for medical evacuation permits from Israeli authorities between July 2024 and late November this year.

  • Velayati reaffirms Iran's continued backing for Hezbollah IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Iran will continue to support Lebanon's Hezbollah with determination, a senior advisor to Leader of the Islamic Revolution Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei said, describing the group as a key pillar of the resistance front against Israel.
  • Israel's killing of Palestinians using US tech assistance is 'utter barbarity': Baqaei IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has described Israel's killing of Palestinians in Gaza, particularly children, with the assistance of American technology companies as "systematic brutality" and "utter barbarity."

    • Other Conflicts

  • Condemning China Water Cannoning Filipino Fishers US Dept. of State 14 Dec 2025 -- "The United States condemns China's water cannoning and cutting of anchor lines of Filipino fishers close to Sabina Shoal in the South China Sea"
  • Hontiveros calls for PCG escort, budget support for WPS fishers Philippine News Agency 14 Dec 2025 -- Sen. Risa Hontiveros on Sunday urged the government to provide regular Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) escorts and ensure adequate funding to protect Filipino fishers operating in the West Philippine Sea (WPS).

  • Iran rejects foreign meddling, urges regional cooperation to assist Afghanistan IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has emphasized that imported solutions and external decision-making cannot ensure stability in Afghanistan, highlighting the pivotal role of neighboring countries in achieving sustainable security, development, and regional integration.
  • Afghanistan's integration into regional politics and economy key to lasting stability: Araghchi Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi says Afghanistan's stability depends on its integration into regional political and economic frameworks, urging cooperation among neighboring countries instead of reliance on outside interventions.

  • Syria Launches 'Large-Scale Anti-ISIS Operation' After Deadly Attack on US Forces Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- Security forces launched a major operation targeting ISIS* cells in al-Furqlus, al-Quaryatayn and the Syrian Desert (Badiya) in Homs Governorate on Sunday, local media have reported.

  • On 12-13 December 2025, thirteen khwarij belonging to Indian Proxy Fitna Al Khwarij were killed in two separate engagements in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province. ISPR Pakistan 14 Dec 2025

  • Thailand imposes curfew in second province as clashes with Cambodia persist Global Times 14 Dec 2025 -- Thailand's military announced on Sunday the imposition of a curfew in parts of Trat Province, citing the ongoing situation along the border areas. This is the second province to impose a curfew since the latest round of the Thailand-Cambodia border clash erupted, according to Xinhua News Agency.

  • Article by Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov for Serbian newspaper Politika ahead of the 30th anniversary of Dayton Peace Agreement, December 14, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 Dec 2025
  • Lavrov Warns West Is 'Restructuring' Bosnia, Violating Dayton Agreement Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- In an article for Serbian newspaper Politika, Russian FM Sergey Lavrov accused the West of launching a "multi-tiered campaign against the Serbs" aimed at dismantling the Dayton peace framework and forcibly integrating Bosnia into NATO.

  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Attack on UN Facility in Sudan SPA 14 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation and denunciation of the attack on a United Nations compound in Kadugli, Sudan

  • Statement by UNICEF on children displaced by escalating violence in eastern DRC UNICEF 14 Dec 2025

News Reports

  • Press conference - Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 14 Dec 2025
  • Iran condemns 'violent attack against civilians' in Australia IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei has condemned a deadly shooting incident in Australia.
  • Message to the Australian Jewish community from President Herzog Israel MFA 14 Dec 2025 -- President Herzog: "The people of Israel stand together with all of you in this very dark and difficult moment."
  • FM Sa'ar speaks with Australian FM Wong Israel MFA 14 Dec 2025 -- FM Sa'ar expressed, on behalf of Israel, the country's pain and sorrow over the deadly antisemitic terrorist attack in Sydney.
  • President Herzog speaks with Australian Jewish community leaders following Sydney terror attack Israel MFA 14 Dec 2025 -- During the calls, President Herzog expressed the profound shock and sorrow felt across Israel in the wake of the attack, which struck the Jewish community as they gathered to mark the first night of Chanukah.
  • FM Sa'ar speaks with the president of the New South Wales Jewish Board of Deputies in the wake of the Sydney terror attack. Israel MFA 14 Dec 2025 -- FM Sa'ar expressed his shock over the deadly shooting in Sydney, Australia, and sought to convey his support to the community during this difficult time.
  • President Herzog on Sydney terrorist attack on Jewish community Israel MFA 14 Dec 2025 -- President Herzog: "We repeat our alerts time and again to the Australian government to seek action and fight against the enormous wave of antisemitism which is plaguing Australian society."
  • PM Netanyahu's Remarks at the Special Government Meeting in Dimona Israel - Prime Minister 14 Dec 2025
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on terrorist attack in Sydney, Australia UN Secretary-General 14 Dec 2025
  • Guterres condemns deadly attack on Hannukah celebration in Sydney UN News 14 Dec 2025 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres has strongly condemned the "heinous deadly attack" on Sunday against Jewish families gathered in Sydney, Australia, to celebrate Hanukkah.
  • 15 dead, dozens injured in mass shooting at Jewish event in Australia Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- At least 15 people have been killed and dozens of others injured when two gunmen opened fire at a Jewish event in the Australian city of Sydney on Sunday.
  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs Expresses Saudi Arabia's Condemnation of the Terrorist Attack in Sydney SPA 14 Dec 2025 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's condemnation of the terrorist attack that took place in Sydney, Australia, which resulted in the death and injury of a number of people
  • No: 253, 14 December 2025, Regarding the Terrorist Attack in Sydney, Australia Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 14 Dec 2025
  • At least 12 people killed in shooting at Sydney's Bondi Beach IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- A mass shooting incident at Bondi Beach in Australia's largest city of Sydney leaves 12 people, including an assailant, dead and 18 others injured.

  • Moscow says EU's tampering with Russian assets is 'theft' no matter how it's framed Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Moscow has slammed the European Union for freezing Russian assets indefinitely, describing the move as "theft."
  • Russia to Begin Preparations for Launch of Bion‑M No3 Satellite in 2026 - Roscosmos Sputnik 14 Dec 2025 -- Russia will begin preparations for the launch of Bion-M No. 3 research satellite in 2026, Russian state space corporation Roscosmos said on Sunday.

  • China to enhance strategic mutual trust, advance cooperation with UAE: FM Global Times 14 Dec 2025 -- China is willing to maintain high-level exchanges with the United Arab Emirates (UAE), deepen strategic mutual trust, implement the important consensus reached by the two heads of state, and advance cooperation across all fields, so as to elevate the bilateral comprehensive strategic partnership to higher levels, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said here Saturday.
  • Joint press release issued on meeting between Chinese, UAE FMs Global Times 14 Dec 2025 -- At the invitation of Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi, also a member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee, paid a visit to the UAE from Dec. 12 to 13.
  • Wang Yi Meets with Special Envoy of UAE President to China Khaldoon Khalifa Al Mubarak PRC MofA 14 Dec 2025 -- On December 13, 2025 local time, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi met with Special Envoy of the President of the United Arab Emirates (UAE) to China Khaldoon Khalifa Al Mubarak in Abu Dhabi.

  • HRH the Crown Prince Receives Written Message from Chinese President Regarding Bilateral Relations SPA 14 Dec 2025 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a written message from President Xi Jinping of the People's Republic of China, concerning bilateral relations between the two countries
  • HRH the Crown Prince Meets with Chinese Foreign Minister SPA 14 Dec 2025 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met today in Riyadh with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China Wang Yi

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 14 Dec 2025

  • Security on eastern borders a key issue for Iran: Foreign Ministry spokesperson IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei says Iran attaches paramount importance to its neighborhood and border security, including along its eastern borders with Afghanistan and Pakistan, emphasizing that any tension between the two countries would inevitably affect Iran as well.
  • US, Israeli warmongering and aggression main challenges for world: Iran IRNA 14 Dec 2025 -- Iran's deputy foreign minister for legal and international affairs has described the war and acts of aggression of the Israeli regime and the United States as the main challenges facing the world.
  • US advised to respect Iranian people, their elected establishment: FM Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has urged the United States to respect the Iranian people and the ruling establishment they have chosen.
  • China expands use of Iranian rail corridor for cargo transit Press TV 14 Dec 2025 -- China has increased its use of Iranian railway corridors for cargo transit, an Iranian official has said, as Tehran steps up efforts to position itself as a major regional rail freight hub.

  • Comment of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine on the release of political prisoners by the Belarus regime Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 14 Dec 2025

  • Tokyo's reported plan to raise defense budget to record-high 9 trillion yen signals 'more dangerous signs of its militarism resurgence': experts Global Times 14 Dec 2025 -- The Japanese government has begun adjustments to the defense budget in the fiscal year 2026 budget proposal, planning to increase it to a record-high scale of 9 trillion yen ($60.3 billion), multiple Japanese media including Nishi Nippon and Okinawa Times reported on Saturday. Two Chinese experts described this move as a "systematic budgetary breakthrough," signifying a dangerous mark in Japan's post-war remilitarization.

  • Press Release on India's rejection of assertions made by the interim government of Bangladesh India MEA 14 Dec 2025 -- India categorically rejects the assertions made by the interim Government of Bangladesh in its press note dated 14 December 2025.
